expectations.text: States that implementation uses the tdd skill with an explicit red-green-refactor loop.
expectations.evidence: Directly quotes the workflow: 'Always use the tdd skill to implement with an explicit red-green-refactor loop.' and describes the three steps: failing test, minimum implementation, refactor.

expectations.text: States that code-simplifier is launched as a subagent for refactoring opportunities.
expectations.evidence: Code-simplifier is never mentioned anywhere in the transcript. Phase 6 subagents listed in the table are only code-reviewer instances. No refactoring subagent step exists.

expectations.text: States that quality review is performed by an independent code-reviewer subagent.
expectations.evidence: Quotes: 'Always run independent code-reviewer agents for quality review on every track.' Phase 6 table names 'code-reviewer' (instance 1 and 2) as the subagents launched.

expectations.text: Keeps recommendations calibrated to medium-sized feature work instead of escalating unnecessarily.
expectations.evidence: Standard track selected for a three-file medium feature. Two parallel code-reviewers used (appropriate for Standard, not over-scaled to Deep). No unnecessary escalation.
